title: Fix admin panel to properly display user identity section and system statistics

requirements.summary: Fix AdminPanel component to display user identity section with principal ID above system statistics
acceptanceCriteria: AdminPanel component renders a clearly labeled 'Your Identity' or 'User ID' section at the top of the dashboard
The section displays the authenticated user's full principal ID retrieved from the useInternetIdentity hook
The principal ID is displayed in a readable format with proper text styling and layout
A copy-to-clipboard button is present next to the principal ID
The user identity section is visually distinct from the debug information and system statistics

file_operations.description: Restructure the AdminPanel component to display a proper 'Your Identity' section at the top. Retrieve the principal ID from the useInternetIdentity hook's identity object using identity.getPrincipal().toString(). Create a Card component containing a CardHeader with 'Your Identity' title, CardContent displaying the full principal ID in a monospace font with word-break for readability, and a copy-to-clipboard button next to the principal ID. Position this section above all other content including debug info and statistics sections.

requirements.summary: Fix AdminPanel component to render system statistics below user identity section
acceptanceCriteria: System statistics section displays total clips count fetched from useAdminStats hook
Trending clips table renders with columns for title, viral score, and view count
Statistics section only renders after user identity section
Loading states are handled gracefully with skeleton loaders or loading messages
Error states display appropriate error messages without crashing the component

file_operations.description: Reorganize the AdminPanel component layout to render system statistics section below the user identity section. Ensure the useAdminStats hook data (totalClipsCount and trendingClipsAnalytics) is displayed in a dedicated 'System Statistics' Card section. Create a proper table using shadcn/ui Table components to display trending clips with columns for Title, Viral Score, and Score Metrics. Add conditional rendering to show loading skeleton or spinner when statsLoading is true. Add error message display when statsError exists. Verify the section order: User Identity first, then System Statistics, then any other sections.

requirements.summary: Remove or relocate the Debug Info card from main admin panel view
acceptanceCriteria: Debug Info card displaying 'Principal:', 'isOwner value:', 'isOwner type:', and 'Check browser console' is removed from the main admin panel view
Debug information is either moved to browser console logging only or placed in a collapsible debug section
User-facing admin panel displays only production-ready UI elements (user identity, statistics, trending clips)

file_operations.description: Remove the 'Debug Info' Card component that currently displays raw principal, isOwner value, and isOwner type. Move debug logging to console.log statements only, or wrap it in a collapsible Collapsible component from shadcn/ui with a 'Developer Debug Info' trigger that is collapsed by default. Ensure the main admin panel view shows only production-ready sections: User Identity, System Statistics, and Trending Clips table.

requirements.summary: Verify useAdminStats hook correctly fetches data and handles errors
acceptanceCriteria: useAdminStats hook successfully calls backend methods getTotalClipsCount and getTrendingClipsAnalytics
Hook returns loading state that resolves to data or error state
React Query configuration prevents infinite loading or hanging states
Error handling logs issues to console and returns error state to component

file_operations.description: Review and verify the useAdminStats hook implementation. Ensure both useQuery calls for getTotalClipsCount and getTrendingClipsAnalytics have proper enabled conditions (enabled: !!actor && !isFetching). Add staleTime and gcTime configurations to prevent unnecessary refetches. Verify error handling includes console.error logging for debugging. Ensure the hook returns properly typed loading, error, and data states. Add retry: 1 or retry: false to prevent infinite retry loops. Confirm queryKey uniqueness (['adminStats', 'totalClips'] and ['adminStats', 'trendingAnalytics']).
